title: Family members list — E2E scenarios
prerequisites: Staging app with test family, location/health mocks or fixtures as needed; Firebase Remote Config overridable in non-prod.
Safety badge matrix from design/KUDO-35 available for visual assertions (or use placeholder states until attached).

test_cases.title: Open family list — layout, ordering, and “You” last
test_cases.preconditions: Signed-in user with ≥3 family members (mix of Safe / Not safe). Include at least one long display name and one with unicode/special characters if available.
steps: Launch the app (or resume cold start) and navigate to the Family members list.
Capture row order and visible content for each member.
Confirm the current user row is labeled as “You” and appears as the last row.
Confirm every row shows an avatar and a single-line name (long names ellipsized, no wrap).
test_cases.expected_result: List loads without error. Not safe members appear above all Safe members; within each group names sort A→Z. “You” is always last. Avatars present; names stay on one line with ellipsis when needed.

test_cases.title: List stays fresh — foreground interval and return from background
test_cases.preconditions: On the list screen; ability to change member data or ordering from backend/fixture while app runs. RC list-refresh interval at default (5 s) unless testing override in the same run with RC staging.
steps: With the app foregrounded on the list, change data that affects order or row content; wait without leaving the screen until the next refresh cycle.
Background the app, apply another change, return to foreground and reopen or land on the list.
Optionally scroll mid-list before refresh and confirm scroll/selection behavior is acceptable (no duplicates, no stuck loading).
test_cases.expected_result: Foreground: list picks up changes on the RC-driven interval (~5 s default). Returning from background: list reloads order and row data without requiring force-quit. UI remains stable across refresh (no duplicate rows / broken scroll per product expectations).

test_cases.title: Safety badge and list reorder after status changes
test_cases.preconditions: Fixture or second device can flip a member between Safe and Not safe; start on list with known states.
steps: Note a member’s badge and position.
Toggle that member to Not safe; wait for refresh.
Toggle back to Safe; wait for refresh.
test_cases.expected_result: Badge matches design for each state. After each change, ordering rules still hold (Not safe block above Safe; A→Z within blocks; You last). No indefinitely stale badge.

test_cases.title: Vehicle vs non-vehicle — speed and health visibility
test_cases.preconditions: One member can be driven in vehicle (>6 km/h, hysteresis satisfied); same or another member walking/stationary with today’s health samples (Apple Health / Health Connect) for multiple metric types.
steps: With the vehicle member in sustained vehicle state, open the list: confirm speed is shown and health summaries are hidden.

With the walking/stationary member, confirm speed is not shown and up to two health indicator types appear for “today” (viewer-local day) with plausible formatted values (steps, sleep, HR, BP, glucose per copy).
If timestamps tie, confirm priority BP → Blood sugar → Sleep → HR → Steps matches implementation (spot-check with fixture).
test_cases.expected_result: Speed only when In a vehicle. Health hidden entirely in vehicle. When not in vehicle, health rules and formatting match product (incl. mmHg spelling, “Blood sugar” label); empty state when no data—no fake numbers.

test_cases.title: Low battery indicator and RC threshold
test_cases.preconditions: Device or mock can set member battery around default RC threshold (10%).
steps: Set battery clearly above threshold: open list — no “Low battery” label.
Set battery clearly below threshold: label appears with exact copy “Low battery”.
If staging allows, change RC threshold and repeat one above/below check.
test_cases.expected_result: Indicator follows RC threshold (default 10%). Label text is exactly “Low battery”. Activity status later stays consistent with low-battery yes/no when member is online.

test_cases.title: Online activity line — place, movement, phone, weather, time, connectivity
test_cases.preconditions: Member online with controllable GPS/precise location, motion, call state, and (if available) weather/time fixtures.
steps: Exercise one representative place branch (e.g. home/saved) and one “other” branch with stayed ≥ RC minutes (default 20) vs never stayed / under threshold.
Exercise walking vs vehicle with hysteresis (RC default 6 s) and no-GPS / precise-off variants as one consolidated pass.
Trigger in-call and active phone-use states; optional severe weather and local late-night window (21:00–05:00) if harness supports.
Confirm activity text uses at most two lines with trimmed overflow.
test_cases.expected_result: Activity string reflects the combined rules for the configured states (per product copy). Walk/vehicle switches respect hysteresis. Layout stays ≤2 lines with ellipsis. No offline template while online.

test_cases.title: Offline — only “No connection, last seen …” variants
test_cases.preconditions: Member can be taken offline with controlled “last seen” offsets (fixtures for <1 min, minutes, hours, days, very old).
steps: For each last-seen band, open the list and read the full activity line.
While offline, confirm no speed, health chips, or extra place/movement fragments appear on the row.
test_cases.expected_result: Only offline family strings: “just now”, “Xm ago”, “Yh ago”, “Zd ago”, or “No connection long time ago” per implementation. No other status fragments. Row extras (speed/health) absent unless product explicitly differs.

test_cases.title: Remote Config drives timing thresholds (single harness pass)
test_cases.preconditions: Non-prod RC or mock exposes list refresh interval, low-battery %, stayed-place minutes, walk/vehicle hysteresis.
steps: Set non-default values for each key (one session).
Observe list refresh cadence, low-battery visibility, stayed-place classification, and walk/vehicle switching delay align with those values.
test_cases.expected_result: Each RC parameter affects the corresponding UI/behavior; reverting to defaults restores default behavior (5 s refresh, 10% battery, 20 min stayed, 6 s hysteresis).

test_cases.title: Smoke — no speed/health leakage in wrong states
test_cases.preconditions: Members in walking, stationary, no-location, and offline states as available.
steps: On each row in those states, assert speed is absent unless vehicle state is truly active.
For offline rows, assert activity is only the offline last-seen pattern.
test_cases.expected_result: No speed when not in vehicle; offline rows do not mix in online activity fragments.
